Legal

Cookie Policy

Last updated: March 2026

Contents

  1. 1. What Are Cookies
  2. 2. Our Approach
  3. 3. Cookies We Use
  4. 4. Google Search Console
  5. 5. No Tracking
  6. 6. Your Choices
  7. 7. Contact

Short version: This website does not use advertising cookies, tracking cookies, or any tool that profiles your behaviour. We collect no analytics about how you navigate the site. Your visit is private.

1. What Are Cookies

Cookies are small text files that a website places on your device when you visit. They are widely used to make websites work efficiently and to provide information to the site owner. Cookies can be set by the website itself ("first-party cookies") or by third-party services embedded in the page ("third-party cookies").

Under EU law — including the ePrivacy Directive and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — cookies that are not strictly necessary require your informed consent before they are placed on your device.

2. Our Approach

We keep our cookie use to an absolute minimum. Axionly.io is a static website with no user accounts, no login sessions, and no advertising. We have deliberately chosen not to embed analytics platforms, advertising networks, or session recording tools.

As a result, this website places no cookies on your device that require your consent. You will not be shown a cookie consent banner because there is nothing to consent to.

3. Cookies We Use

Category In Use Details
Strictly Necessary None The site is fully static. No session or authentication cookies are required.
Analytics & Performance None We do not use Google Analytics, Plausible, Matomo, or any visitor analytics platform.
Functional None No preference or personalisation cookies are set. Language selection is handled via URL path only.
Targeting & Advertising None No advertising pixels, retargeting scripts, or social media trackers are present.

4. Google Search Console

We use Google Search Console — a free tool provided by Google — to understand how our website performs in Google Search results. This includes data such as which search queries lead users to our site, how our pages are indexed, and whether there are any technical issues affecting our visibility.

Google Search Console does not place any cookies on website visitors' browsers. It operates entirely within Google's own infrastructure and the data it surfaces to us is aggregated and anonymised — we cannot identify individual users from it. It is a tool for the site administrator, not a visitor tracking mechanism.

For more information on how Google handles data in its tools, see Google's Privacy Policy.

5. No Behavioural Tracking

We do not track, record, or analyse how individual visitors navigate this website. Specifically, we do not use:

  • Google Analytics or similar visitor analytics platforms
  • Session recording or heatmap tools (e.g. Hotjar, Microsoft Clarity)
  • Facebook Pixel or other advertising network trackers
  • LinkedIn Insight Tag or any social media pixel
  • A/B testing or personalisation tools that observe user behaviour

We made this choice deliberately. As a company that advises organisations on AI governance and data privacy, we hold ourselves to the same standards we recommend to our clients.

6. Your Choices

Because this website places no non-essential cookies, there is nothing to opt out of. You can use this site without any impact from cookie-related choices.

If you nonetheless wish to control cookies at the browser level, all major browsers allow you to view, block, or delete cookies. Refer to your browser's help documentation:

  • Google Chrome
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Apple Safari
  • Microsoft Edge

7. Contact

If you have any questions about this Cookie Policy or how we handle your data, please contact us:

Axiontic SRL — Axionly

Aleea Lavandei 7B, Oradea, BH 410083, Romania

CUI: 44913578 · VAT EU: 46009350

hello@axionly.io

See also our Privacy Policy for full details on how we collect and protect your personal data.

This policy may be updated from time to time. The date at the top of the page reflects when it was last revised.
